attributes.content: Complete entire sequence at same institution prior to transfer

notes.content: The Department of Economics at UC Davis trains students from diverse backgrounds in pursuit of solutions to society’s most pressing economic problems using research and interdisciplinary ideas at the frontier of knowledge. Economics majors all complete core courses that include principles, intermediate theory, quantitative methods and economic history. In addition to these core courses, students are free to take a variety of classes according to their own individual interests across the discipline. Some students choose to specialize in behavior and strategy, data analytics and economic analysis, economic history, international macro-finance, policy, or poverty and inequality.
notes.contentHtml:
The Department of Economics at UC Davis trains students from diverse backgrounds in pursuit of solutions to society’s most pressing economic problems using research and interdisciplinary ideas at the frontier of knowledge.


Economics majors all complete core courses that include principles, intermediate theory, quantitative methods and economic history. In addition to these core courses, students are free to take a variety of classes according to their own individual interests across the discipline. Some students choose to specialize in behavior and strategy, data analytics and economic analysis, economic history, international macro-finance, policy, or poverty and inequality.

notes.content: Internships for economics majors have been arranged at banks, brokerages, other business enterprises, and governmental units. The internships must complement the student's course work. A degree in economics is excellent preparation for students who want to go on to law school, business school, advanced work in economics, or graduate work in international relations. It is also a good background for careers in management and positions with the government.
notes.contentHtml: Internships for economics majors have been arranged at banks, brokerages, other business enterprises, and governmental units. The internships must complement the student's course work. A degree in economics is excellent preparation for students who want to go on to law school, business school, advanced work in economics, or graduate work in international relations. It is also a good background for careers in management and positions with the government.

notes.content: This is a competitive major and is subject to the Selective Major Review process. Students need to take all courses for a letter grade with no grade less than C and complete all major preparatory course requirements by the end of spring term prior to their fall enrollment. All students must meet a 2.8 GPA cumulative GPA for admission. Important Information: Complete the courses below and maintain a 2.50 GPA across the entire group: ● Economics (ECN) 001A/B ● Mathematics (MAT) 017A/B or 019A/B* or 021A/B *Starting Fall 2027 students must complete the whole MAT 019A/B/ C series. Visit Department agreements as needed Contact your UC Davis Advisor for questions ( Find my advisor ) Note: Advanced Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) Examination: AP and IB examination credit policies are detailed in the UC Davis General Catalog . Quick reference charts for AP and IB.
notes.contentHtml:
This is a competitive major and is subject to the Selective Major Review process. Students need to take all courses for a letter grade with no grade less than C and complete all major preparatory course requirements by the end of spring term prior to their fall enrollment. All students must meet a 2.8 GPA cumulative GPA for admission.

 

Important Information:


Complete the courses below and maintain a 2.50 GPA across the entire group:

●    Economics (ECN) 001A/B

●    Mathematics (MAT) 017A/B or 019A/B* or 021A/B


*Starting Fall 2027 students must complete the whole MAT 019A/B/C series.


Visit Department agreements as needed


Contact your UC Davis Advisor for questions (Find my advisor)


Note:

Advanced Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) Examination:

AP and IB examination credit policies are detailed in the UC Davis General Catalog. Quick reference charts for AP and IB.

notes.content: At the time of TAG submission, Economics requires a minimum 3.20 cumulative GPA and at least a 2.50 GPA across the entire group of courses with no grades less than a C to receive a Transfer Admission Guarantee.
